danger_signals.meaning: Imminent defeat; player is in danger of losing the match

danger_signals.visual_form: Creature attack animations, gory finishing moves
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat from opponent; danger of taking damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Ruined stages with barren landscapes, volcanic areas (Inferno), tombs, decay
danger_signals.meaning: World is inherently hostile and unstable; danger permeates the environment

danger_signals.visual_form: Darker color palettes, more aggressive animations, tyrannosaur/demonic forms
danger_signals.meaning: Chaotic and destructive threat to world stability

safety_signals.meaning: Player character is safe and has combat advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Lighter color palettes, ape-like and protective creature forms
safety_signals.meaning: Forces of peace and preservation; represent stability

safety_signals.visual_form: Human tribesmen visible in background, moving peacefully
safety_signals.meaning: Civilization and order exist; safe worship space

safety_signals.visual_form: Natural environments associated with good-aligned creatures
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones spiritually aligned with protective gods

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cliff (Blizzard domain), Hollows (Armadon domain), Strip (Talon domain)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Tyrannosaur (Sauron, Diablo), ape-like decay form (Chaos), cobra (Vertigo)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: More aggressive attack animations, gory finishing moves
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cove (Sauron domain), Ruins (Chaos domain), Tomb (Vertigo domain), Inferno (Diablo domain)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Vertigo as cobra goddess of insanity; straddling good/evil
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Humorous and unpredictable finishing moves (La Vache Qui Rit transformation)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tomb domain suggests ancient, ambiguous power

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ruined landscapes, barren stages, volcanic Inferno, shifted continental landmass shaped like fire-breathing dinosaur skull
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Seven creatures emerge as gods to fill power vacuum; human civilization regresses to tribal worship

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Good gods vs. evil gods occupy opposing stage domains with distinct visual theming
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creatures battle to determine planet's fate; humans form segregated clans beneath their chosen deity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Evil-aligned stages (Ruins, Inferno) feature darker, more chaotic visuals; Chaos god of decay represented
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Evil gods attempt to plunge world into further chaos; worshippers wander as vulnerable victims

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Good-aligned stages (Cliff, Hollows, Strip) feature natural, stable environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Good gods keep peace; worshippers safely gather to worship

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Minigame where player consumes worshippers to increase health bar before final battle
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creature must consume followers to gain power for ultimate conflict; visual representation of desperation

environmental_storytelling.example: Blizzard's Cliff domain suggests cold, isolated protection; Diablo's Inferno suggests hellish corruption; visual environment reinforces character alignment
Human tribesmen wander stages as passive observers, can be consumed or tossed by creatures; visual metaphor for humans as powerless subjects of god-monsters
Good-aligned creatures use lighter palettes; evil-aligned use darker, warmer tones; visual shorthand for moral alignment without dialogue
Ape-like protectors (Blizzard, Chaos) vs. predatory tyrannosaurs (Sauron, Diablo) vs. specialized survival forms (Talon raptor, Armadon ceratopian); body type signals worldview
Gory, violent finishers on evil gods; humorous transformations (Vertigo's cow spell) suggest moral complexity; visual storytelling of character nature

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Protective pose; worshippers remain safe around creature; domain environment stabilizes visually
Aggressive dominance display; worshippers flee or cower; environment darkens or becomes chaotic
Damaged animation frames increase in frequency; movement becomes erratic; visual signs of weakening
Peaceful wandering, passive worship animations near benevolent god stages
Can be tossed, consumed, or devoured; visual vulnerability; flee from aggressive creatures
Unpredictable finishing moves (transformation finisher); cobra form suggests cunning; visual ambiguity matches insanity theme

notes: Primal Rage uses creature mythology, color symbolism, and environmental theming to communicate a post-apocalyptic world split between chaos and order. The seven gods form two opposing moral factions (good and evil) plus one neutral/unpredictable element (Vertigo). Worshippers serve as visual indicators of world stability—their safety and worship behavior reflects current god's alignment. The game's visual communication relies heavily on creature morphology (predator vs. protector forms), color palette shifts (light vs. dark gods), and stage domain aesthetics rather than explicit UI or dialogue. The consumption minigame before the final battle uses visual mechanics to communicate desperation and survival instinct. Finishing moves function as both mechanical reward and moral commentary—gory violence for destructive gods, humorous transformation for the chaos-god.
